description: Working on customer authentication and account lifecycle for 8M+ B2B and B2C customer accounts, built with Kotlin/Spring Boot on Keycloak / Red Hat SSO.
items: Built the customer authentication and account lifecycle services for 8M+ customer accounts (B2B and B2C) — account creation, email verification, password reset, login, logout, recovery, profile updates, and deletion — on Kotlin/Spring Boot with Keycloak / Red Hat SSO.
Migrated and consolidated B2B and B2C accounts into a single Keycloak layer — one source of truth for customer profiles that downstream personalisation and segmentation services rely on.
Cut p95 latency by ~20% on the busiest paths (authentication and account lookups) by rewriting the slowest PostgreSQL queries and adding composite indexes.
Reworked the Gradle build to use multi-level CI caching, cutting build times by ~35%.
results: Wrote a C4-model architecture proof of concept in Structurizr DSL that seeded the team's architecture documentation.
The Gradle build optimisation was picked up as an internal reference by multiple teams company-wide.
selectedWorkBlurb: Customer authentication and account lifecycle for 8M+ B2B/B2C accounts — Kotlin + Spring Boot on Keycloak / Red Hat SSO.

description: Short engagement building a data quality tool that scanned Dassault's global online sales and product-referential systems for missing fields, broken references, and inconsistent values across millions of records.
items: Built a data quality tool that scanned Dassault’s global online sales and product-referential systems for missing fields, broken references, and inconsistent values across millions of records.

Started this tool as a Python/SQL prototype, then rewrote it as a Java/Spring Boot service integrated into the release pipeline, partnering with business analysts to translate vague data-quality rules into automated checks.

company.name: Audi AG, Ingolstadt — via Lunatech SAS

description: Built Audi's in-house ground-truth labelling platform and data pipeline for training autonomous-driving models. Worked remotely from Paris on a fast-growing team.
items: Built Audi's in-house ground-truth labelling platform and data pipeline for training autonomous-driving models — 5M+ labelled frames from 2,000 driving videos, with automated validation keeping label accuracy at 98%+.
Integrated Argo Workflows for batch and async pipelines that pre-labelled data with model predictions, roughly doubling annotation throughput for the ML teams.
Streamed labelling and annotation events through Kafka, indexed them into Elasticsearch to power dataset search, slicing, and quality dashboards used by ML and QA teams.
Invested early in the backend architecture so the codebase held up as the team grew — other engineers could build on top of it and ship features ~40% faster.
results: The early architecture held through the entire project — scaling from prototype to 5M+ frames and 10+ engineers with no major rewrite required.
selectedWorkBlurb: Built Audi's in-house labelling platform and data pipeline — 5M+ ground-truth frames at 98%+ accuracy, feeding autonomous-driving model training.

description: Helped lead the technical pilot for the new marketing platform integrating Tridion CMS with the Disneyland Paris public site (~10 million annual visitors).
items: Helped lead the technical pilot for the new marketing platform integrating Tridion CMS with the Disneyland Paris public site (~10 million annual visitors).
The pilot succeeded well enough that Disney funded D4th (Disney 4th gen) — a €22M+ programme to fully replace the legacy marketing application, scoped off the pilot.
Built supporting microservices (such as browser push notifications) and a SOAP interface towards legacy internal systems.
results: Took ownership of the pilot's supporting services and built the GitLab CI/CD pipeline that took them live for D4th — later used by a senior infrastructure engineer to deploy the rest of the D4th system.
selectedWorkBlurb: Helped lead the technical pilot for the new marketing platform integrating Tridion CMS with the public site (~10M annual visitors). The pilot grew into D4th — a €22M+ replacement of the legacy app.

description: Worked remotely on a B2B shipment-lifecycle platform — new features, maintenance, and day-to-day operations.
items: Added a Quartz-based scheduler that pushed automated reports to operations so delivery issues could be caught earlier.
Deployed to AWS EC2 and handled day-to-day production: log analysis and incident triage.

company.name: Nokia Bell Labs (formerly Alcatel-Lucent) — Paris, France

description: Internship in the research division working on video-quality estimation and streaming protocols.
items: Built a video-quality estimation tool in Java implementing the ITU-T P.1201.1 model.
Analysed streaming traffic with Wireshark and tcpdump as part of research into QUIC, HTTP/2, and H.264.
